title: betula alba subsp. glutinosa
intro: Betula alba subsp. glutinosa, also known as Wych elm, is a subspecies of the white elm tree (Betula alba) native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This deciduous tree has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which make it useful in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Betula alba subsp. glutinosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and boost the immune system, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and its bark and leaves are also used in traditional medicine to create salves, poultices, and other topical preparations.
